industries.name: Hardening Alloys & Electrical Contacts
uses: Osmium-iridium (Os-Ir) alloy — historically used for fountain pen nib tips and stylus tips for record players — the hardest of all natural alloys. Virtually obsolete in fountain pens (now replaced by synthetic iridium), but Os-Ir tips remain in some premium instruments
Osmium alloys for electrical contacts in telecommunications relays and high-reliability switching equipment where wear resistance under repeated contact cycling is required
Osmium is the densest naturally occurring element (22.59 g/cm³) — occasional specialty uses exploit this extreme density in counterweights and inertial sensors
Platinum-osmium alloys for implantable electrodes, pacemaker tip electrodes, and neural interface contacts — osmium's biocompatibility and hardness are useful in miniaturized electrode applications

companies.name: Johnson Matthey
companies.use: Major PGM refiner recovering osmium as a byproduct of platinum and palladium refining from South African and Russian PGM ores. Produces osmium metal and Os-Ir alloy for specialty applications. World osmium supply is only ~1 tonne per year.

companies.name: Heraeus
companies.use: Refines and supplies osmium metal and osmium compounds for specialty industrial, scientific, and chemical applications. Produces Os-Ir alloy for specialty electrical contacts and instruments.

industries.name: Chemical Synthesis & Staining
uses: Osmium tetroxide (OsO₄): powerful oxidizing agent for cis-dihydroxylation of alkene double bonds in organic synthesis — produces vicinal diols with high stereospecificity. Catalytic Sharpless asymmetric dihydroxylation (Nobel Prize 2001) uses OsO₄ with a chiral ligand

Osmium tetroxide biological staining: OsO₄ fixes and stains lipid membranes in transmission electron microscopy (TEM) — the standard preparation for visualizing cell membranes, organelles, and myelin sheaths in biological ultrastructure studies
Osmium tetroxide as fingerprint developer in forensic science — reacts with fatty acid residues in latent fingerprints producing a dark deposit visible on most surfaces
Osmium is extremely toxic as OsO₄ (volatile, attacks mucous membranes) — all OsO₄ handling requires specialized fume hoods and extreme caution

companies.name: Sigma-Aldrich (Merck KGaA)
companies.use: Major supplier of osmium tetroxide for organic synthesis, biological electron microscopy staining, and research applications. Supplies OsO₄ solutions and crystals to pharmaceutical companies, universities, and research institutions globally.

companies.name: Electron Microscopy Sciences
companies.use: Specialist supplier of electron microscopy preparation reagents including osmium tetroxide fixative solutions for biological and materials science TEM sample preparation.
